zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：空头延续下的弱势震荡
zh.content:
当前AVAXUSD延续弱势下行结构，价格运行于6.31附近，已跌破20日均线6.54，短期趋势偏空，市场动能不足，多头承压明显态。

技术指标显示MACD为-0.1244，动能柱持续位于零轴下方，短线空头占优；RSI缺失但结合均线下穿20日线，整体弱势结构未改。

当前支撑关注6.30整数关口及前低区域，若失守可能下探6.20一带；上方阻力位于6.50-6.55区间，对应20日均线压制。

操作上建议维持偏空思路，反弹至6.50附近可考虑分批做空，止损设于6.60上方；若跌破6.30则顺势加仓，目标6.20附近。

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD shows a short-term bearish bias with price trading below the 20-day SMA at 6.54036. The market has drifted lower with limited volatility, printing a tight 24-hour range near 6.31. This structure suggests consolidation after a gradual downside move, with sellers maintaining control. Price action continues to struggle to reclaim the moving average, reinforcing a weak momentum phase and a lack of sustained bullish follow-through in recent sessions.

The MACD reading at -0.12449514 confirms negative momentum, indicating continued bearish pressure despite the absence of a visible signal line value. With RSI unavailable, price behavior becomes more important, and the inability to push higher suggests subdued buying strength. The flat intraday movement near session lows implies weakening demand, while momentum conditions collectively point toward cautious sentiment and limited upside conviction in the short term.

Immediate support is located around 6.30, a psychological and intraday low zone currently being tested. A breakdown below this level could expose 6.20 as the next meaningful support region. On the upside, initial resistance stands at 6.54 aligned with the SMA20, followed by stronger resistance near 6.70 where prior reaction highs may sit. The compression between support and resistance suggests an imminent directional expansion once volume increases.

Traders may consider a defensive stance while price remains below 6.54, favoring short-term bearish setups with tight risk control above resistance. A break and sustained close above the SMA20 would signal potential reversal and open upside continuation toward 6.70. Until then, range-trading or bearish continuation strategies remain more favorable, with attention to volatility expansion from the current consolidation zone.
